explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the controller is in PCV mode, is currently in the expiratory phase, does not detect a spontaneous breath (meaning drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV is false), and the Expiratory Pause button is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall eventually start (F) within a specified duration (N_DURATION). N_DURATION represents the 'expiration time' mentioned in the prompt, indicating the pause must start within this timeframe.

The property states that if the system is in PCV mode, currently in the expiratory phase, does not detect a spontaneous breath, AND the expiratory pause is set by the GUI, then an expiratory pause must eventually start within the expiration time. This maps to a global implication where the premise combines all initiating conditions (PCVMode, expiratoryPhase, no spontaneous breath, expiratoryPauseButton) and the consequence is that expiratoryPause will eventually be true within a bounded time (N_DURATION) representing the 'expiration time'. Template 34, G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)), perfectly captures this 'always, if condition, then eventually in bounded future' pattern. N_DURATION is set to 1 to represent a generic positive duration for 'within the expiration time'.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the controller is in the PCV mode (PCVMode) AND in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase) AND it does not detect a spontaneous breath (!dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV), AND at the same time the Expiratory Pause start is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then in the very next state (X), an Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPause) shall start. This captures the nested conditional requirement and the immediate effect of the GUI input under specific system conditions. The phrase "within the expiration time" is covered by the condition 'expiratoryPhase' being part of the antecedent.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode, the expiratory phase is active, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' is false), and the 'Expiratory Pause' is initiated by the GUI, then 'Expiratory Pause' shall start in the next time step (X). The phrase 'within the expiration time' is implicitly covered by the 'expiratoryPhase' being part of the antecedent, indicating that the conditions for the event to occur are met during the expiration phase.

The property states that globally (G), if the controller is in PCV Mode (PCVMode), currently in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase), and a spontaneous breath is not detected (!dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV), AND the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then in the next state (X), an Expiratory Pause shall start (expiratoryPause). Template 41 directly captures this 'always if A and B then next C' structure, where A is a combination of conditions and B is the triggering event.

This captures the immediate cause-and-effect relationship described.

This captures the conditional guarantee for the Expiratory Pause in PCV Mode under the given circumstances.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode, and simultaneously the controller is in the expiratory phase, no spontaneous breath is detected (i.e., 'drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV' is false), and the 'Expiratory Pause' button is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall commence within a specified short duration (N_DURATION=1).
